paper_title: Morphological Study of the Alimentary Canal and Malpighian Tubules in the Adult of the Pollen Beetle Meligethes (Odonthogethes) chinensis

methods.method_name: Study on the Structure and Function of Midgut 'Saccate Protrusions'
methods.description: This method involves the morphological and functional study of the midgut 'saccate protrusions' in beetles, particularly focusing on their naming and classification. The study aims to clarify the terminology and understand the structure and function of these protrusions using advanced techniques.

steps.description: In morphological studies of the alimentary canal, the terms of Snodgrass (1935) are mainly used.
steps.searchable_summary: use Snodgrass (1935) terminology for morphological analysis

steps.description: For example, the 'saccate protrusions' on the surface of the midgut of the beetles cannot be clearly distinguished from comparative morphological or functional morphological studies, and the naming is very confusing.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and clarify naming of 'saccate protrusions'

steps.description: In the subsequent study of the digestive system of the subfamily Meligethinae, in order to use the terminology more accurately, it is necessary to use 'transmission electron microscopy' and 'histoenzymology' to further explore the structure and function.
steps.searchable_summary: use transmission electron microscopy and histoenzymology for detailed study
